skill.name: viral-short-form
skill.description: Brainstorm and write high-retention short-form video and carousel content for TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Shorts. Use whenever someone wants viral hook ideas, a video script or outline, content concepts for a product or topic, or wants to critique and improve a draft hook or script. Works for any short-form format: talking head, demo, unboxing, before/after, tutorial, storytime, listicle, carousel, or meme. Produces several diverse hook options from proven patterns, structures scripts for retention (hook, escalation, payoff, CTA), and adapts to each platform. Pattern-based guidance grounded in how short-form tends to perform; it improves the odds, it does not guarantee virality.
skill.summary: Generate hooks, scripts, and carousels for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts using proven retention patterns.
skill.author: vyralcontent

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 73 potential patterns (external_commands, filesystem traversal, weak crypto, hardcoded URLs, system recon). After full review of all 10 files (701 lines), every finding is a false positive: the skill contains only markdown documentation about short-form video content creation. Backticks are markdown formatting, not shell execution. Path traversal patterns are relative link references in markdown. Weak crypto and system recon flags are keyword matches in prose (e.g. 'weak hook', 'system reconnaissance' phrases). No executable code, no scripts, no network calls exist in the skill. The skill is pure instructional content for content creators with no security risk.

content.user_title: Write Viral Short-Form Content with Proven Hooks
content.value_statement: Creators struggle with blank pages and weak hooks that cause viewers to swipe away. This skill provides pattern-based frameworks for hooks, retention structure, and platform-specific scripts that hold attention and earn saves.
actual_capabilities: Generate 6-10 diverse hook options labeled by archetype (curiosity gap, withhold, stakes, contrarian, listicle, etc.)
Structure complete video scripts using the hook, escalation, payoff, CTA retention framework
Draft slide-by-slide carousels for Instagram and LinkedIn with one idea per slide
Critique existing hooks and scripts against three common failure modes with specific fix suggestions
Adapt content for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts with platform-specific formatting
limitations: Pattern-matching guidance only, does not predict or guarantee virality
No access to real viral videos, creator data, or platform analytics
Cannot generate visual assets, video edits, or audio content
Requires user to provide the initial brief, product, or topic context

use_cases.title: Content creators launching products on short-form video
use_cases.description: Get 8-10 hook ideas for a product launch video, then build the strongest into a full retention-structured script ready to film.
use_cases.target_user: TikTok and Reels creators promoting physical products

use_cases.title: Marketers repurposing long-form content into shorts
use_cases.description: Turn a blog post, podcast episode, or YouTube video into several distinct short-form pieces with different hooks and formats.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams with existing long-form content libraries

use_cases.title: Beginner creators learning short-form structure
use_cases.description: Learn the hook-escalation-payoff-CTA framework and diagnose why drafts lose viewers using the three failure modes checklist.
use_cases.target_user: New creators building their first viral video strategy

prompt_templates.title: Generate hooks for a product launch
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick hook brainstorm for a new product video
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me 8 hooks for a short-form video launching my $39 reusable cold-brew maker. Label each by archetype.

prompt_templates.title: Write a complete talking-head script
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a 30-second talking-head script on why most morning routines fail. Include hook, escalation, payoff, and CTA. Give me 3 hook options to choose from first.

prompt_templates.title: Critique and improve my draft hook
prompt_templates.scenario: Get specific feedback on a weak opening
prompt_templates.prompt: Here's my script hook: 'Hey guys, today I'm going to show you this amazing product.' Tell me exactly where it loses people and rewrite it stronger.

prompt_templates.title: Repurpose content into multiple shorts
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn one idea into several distinct pieces
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this blog post about productivity myths into 5 distinct short-form videos: one talking head, one listicle, one carousel, one demo, and one POV. Each needs a different hook.

output_examples.input: Give me hooks for a video launching a $39 reusable cold-brew maker.
output: Withhold: 'I found the only coffee gadget I'd actually rebuy. I'm not saying the brand yet.'
Cost confession: 'I spent $400 on coffee gear so you can skip to the $39 one.'
Contrarian: 'Your expensive coffee setup is why it tastes worse.'
Stakes: 'Stop buying coffee pods. This $39 swap pays for itself in 3 weeks.'
Listicle: '3 reasons your home coffee tastes like barista-level now.'
Direct call-out: 'If your morning coffee costs more than $1, watch this.'

output_examples.input: Write a 30-second talking-head script on why most morning routines fail.
output: Hook: 'Your 5 AM morning routine is why you're exhausted by noon.'
Escalation: 'You copied an influencer's routine. Their life doesn't match yours. So you fight your biology and crash before lunch.'
Payoff: 'The fix: match your routine to your chronotype. Night owls forcing 5 AM wake-ups create cortisol spikes that destroy afternoon focus.'
CTA: 'Save this and check your chronotype tonight. Follow for the part 2 on building a routine that actually fits.'

best_practices: Always offer 6-10 diverse hook options across different archetypes, never just one or variations of the same idea
Lead every hook with the payoff or the tension, not setup, throat-clearing, or 'hey guys'
Match the hook promise to the video payoff, orphan clickbait destroys trust and saves
anti_patterns: Returning a single hook idea instead of multiple archetypes for the user to choose from
Leading with the brand name instead of the viewer's outcome or tension
Promising virality or guaranteed view counts instead of honest pattern-based guidance

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee my video will go viral?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides pattern-based guidance grounded in how short-form content tends to perform. It improves the odds and gives you proven structure to start from, but no tool can predict or guarantee virality.

faq.question: What formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: Talking head, demo, unboxing, before/after, tutorial, storytime, listicle, carousel, meme, and POV. Each format has a dedicated structure playbook in the references.

faq.question: Which platforms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. Each platform has specific guidance for length, sound, captions, safe zones, and distribution heuristics.

faq.question: Can this skill improve my existing script?
faq.answer: Yes. Use critique mode to get diagnosed against the three failure modes: weak hook, no escalation, and buried payoff. The skill identifies exactly where attention drops and rewrites the weak beats.

faq.question: Does this skill need my analytics or account data?
faq.answer: No. The skill works from patterns only. You provide the product, topic, or draft, and it returns structured hooks and scripts. It does not access any external services or your platform accounts.

faq.question: Is the skill free or paid?
faq.answer: This skill is free and open source under MIT license. It uses general patterns. The author's paid product (Vyral) offers grounded outputs based on real viral videos in your niche.
